message.content:
I've addressed both blocking concerns. Here's what changed in commit `e55c052`: ## Fix 1: Empty nav landmark (Heydon's a11y concern) - Moved "Create account" and "Sign in" links into `{% block nav %}` so the header `
` is no longer empty - Removed the separate `
` from the content block ## Fix 2: Missing CSS (Steve Schoger + Lea's concern) - Added `.landing-hero` component in the CSS components layer - 24rem max-width matching auth-form for visual consistency - `.landing-hero__cta` -- full-width primary CTA link styled as button (primary color, rounded, medium weight) - `.landing-hero__alt` -- secondary "Already have an account? Sign in" text in muted small type - All values reference design tokens (no hardcoded values) - `prefers-reduced-motion` respected on CTA transition ## Template structure The content area now has: - `

Welcome

` (unchanged, existing test still passes) - `

Keep track of what you need to do.

` - Primary CTA: "Create account" link to /register - Secondary: "Already have an account? Sign in" link to /login ## Pipeline - `cargo fmt --check` -- PASS - `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` -- PASS - `cargo test` -- 67 integration + 30 unit tests all pass - Working tree clean Please re-review and confirm consensus.

content.text: Good, Luca addressed the a11y and CSS concerns. Let me re-read the updated files to verify the changes don't introduce any TDD or design simplicity issues.

content.content:
1→{% extends "base.html" %} 2→ 3→{% block title %}Todo List{% endblock %} 4→ 5→{% block nav %} 6→Create account 7→Sign in 8→{% endblock %} 9→ 10→{% block content %} 11→
12→

19→{% endblock %} 20→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

content.text: The changes are clean. The handler and tests are unchanged from my previous review -- only the template and CSS were updated to address the a11y and design concerns. Let me confirm my continued consent.

input.content:
Kent here. Re-reviewed the updated commit `e55c052`.

The handler (`src/routes/index.rs`) and tests (`tests/api/index.rs`) are unchanged from my previous review -- still clean, still pass all four rules of simple design. The template changes are purely structural (moving links into the nav block, adding CSS classes) and don't affect the behavioral contracts tested.

All existing tests still pass, which confirms the refactoring didn't break behavior. That's exactly what tests are for -- confidence during change.

**CONSENT confirmed.** My previous review stands. No new TDD or design concerns.
